title: seus-mbon-zooplankton-microscopy
text:
seus-mbon-zooplankton-microscopy
Analyses of SouthEast US MBON research cruise zooplankton microscopy data.

title: MBON Zooplankton Quick Visual
text: This is a reproduced examples from google colab by Tylar

title: MBON Zooplankton Quick Visual
text:
3.1 Print Information
Information for station and site names, mesh sizes and life stages


Code
print(paste("stations:", paste(unique(dat$station), collapse = ", ")))


[1] "stations: Molasses Reef, Looe Key (deep), Western Sambo"


Code
print(paste("stations:", paste(unique(dat$site), collapse = ", ")))


[1] "stations: MR, LK, WS"


Code
print(paste("mesh sizes available: ", paste(unique(dat$mesh), collapse = ", ")))


[1] "mesh sizes available:  500, 200, 64"


Code
print(paste("life stages:", paste(unique(dat$lifeStage), collapse = ", ")))


[1] "life stages: larvae, NA, eggs, nauplii, zoea, megalopa, protozoea, larva"


Code
dat_nest <- dat %>%
  nest(data = c(taxa:last_col()))
count(dat_nest, station, site)

title: MBON Zooplankton Quick Visual
section: 6.1 Plot the Ind/m^3 based on Year
text:
6.1 Plot the Ind/m^3 based on Year
Either sum or mean


Code
# === Iterate over unique stations and create stacked bar charts
dat_filtered %>%
  summarise(
    .by = c(taxa_level, site, year),
    # ind_m3 = mean(ind_m3),
    ind_m3 = sum(ind_m3),
    
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    taxa_level = fct_reorder(taxa_level, ind_m3, .fun = max)
  ) %>%
  ggplot(
    aes(x = year, y = ind_m3, fill = taxa_level,
    color = after_scale(prismatic::clr_darken(fill, 0.3))
        ),
    ) +
  geom_col() +
  labs(
    y = NULL,
    x = "Year",
    fill  = "Taxa Name",
    title = glue("`{const$taxa_lvl[1]}` for Year for Station {const$site} (mesh size {const$mesh})")
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0, 0)) +
  scale_fill_viridis_d(option = "A") +
  guides(fill = guide_legend(ncol = 2, byrow = TRUE)) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(
    legend.box.background = element_rect(color = "grey")
  )

title: MBON Zooplankton Quick Visual
section: 6.2 Plot the Ind/m^3 based on Month and Year
text:
6.2 Plot the Ind/m^3 based on Month and Year
Either sum or mean


Code
dat_filtered %>%
    summarise(
    .by = c(site, year, date_month, taxa_level),
    # ind_m3 = mean(ind_m3)
    ind_m3 = sum(ind_m3)
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    .by     = c(site, date_month),
    total   = sum(ind_m3),
    percent = ind_m3 / total
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    taxa_level = fct_reorder(taxa_level, percent, .fun = max),
    date_month2 = fct_reorder(format(date_month, "%b %Y"), date_month)
  ) %>%
  ggplot(
    aes(x = date_month2, y = ind_m3, fill = taxa_level, 
        color = after_scale(prismatic::clr_darken(fill, 0.3))
        )
         ) +
  geom_col() +
  labs(
    y = NULL,
    x = "Year",
    fill  = "Taxa Name",
    title = glue("`{const$taxa_lvl[1]}` for Year for Station {const$site} (mesh size {const$mesh})")
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    expand = c(0, 0)
  ) +
  scale_fill_viridis_d(option = "A") +
  guides(fill = guide_legend(ncol = 2, byrow = TRUE)) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(
    legend.box.background = element_rect(color = "grey"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)
  )

title: MBON Zooplankton Quick Visual
section: 6.2 Plot the Percent (%) Ind/m^3 based on Month and Year
text:
6.2 Plot the Percent (%) Ind/m^3 based on Month and Year
Either sum or mean


Code
dat_filtered %>%
    summarise(
    .by = c(site, year, date_month, taxa_level),
    # ind_m3 = mean(ind_m3)
    ind_m3 = sum(ind_m3)
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    .by     = c(site, date_month),
    total   = sum(ind_m3),
    percent = ind_m3 / total
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    taxa_level = fct_reorder(taxa_level, percent, .fun = max),
    date_month2 = fct_reorder(format(date_month, "%b %Y"), date_month)
  ) %>%
  ggplot(
    aes(x = date_month2, 
        y = percent, 
        fill  = taxa_level, 
        color = after_scale(prismatic::clr_darken(fill, 0.3))
        )
         ) +
  geom_col() +
  labs(
    y = NULL,
    x = NULL,
    fill  = "Taxa Name",
    title = glue("`{const$taxa_lvl[1]}` for Cruise for Station {const$site} (mesh size {const$mesh})")
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    expand = c(0, 0),
    labels = scales::label_percent()
  ) +
  scale_fill_viridis_d(option = "A") +
  guides(fill = guide_legend(ncol = 2, byrow = TRUE)) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(
    legend.box.background = element_rect(color = "grey"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)
  )

title: MBON Zooplankton Quick Visual
text:
7.1 200 μm


Code
dat_lowest_200 <-
  dat %>%
  filter(
    mesh == 200           # mesh size
    & is.na(lifeStage)      # assumed na is adult
  )  %>% 
  mutate(
    site = fct(site, c("MR", "LK", "WS")),
    station = fct(station, c("Molasses Reef", "Looe Key (deep)", "Western Sambo")), 
    # select the lowest taxa name available
    taxa_level = pmap_chr(
      across(c(const$taxa_lvl)),
      \(...) {
        dat <- c(...) # combine all names into a vector
        taxa_name <- NA
        # loop through all names to select the lowest name
        for (i in seq(length(dat))) {
          if (is.na(taxa_name)) {
            taxa_name <- dat[i]
          } else {
            taxa_name
          }
        }
        return(taxa_name)
      }
    ),
  ) %T>%
  print()

   cruise_id station site   mesh date_time           date        year date_month
   <chr>     <fct>   <fct> <dbl> <dttm>              <date>     <dbl> <date>    
 1 WS17170   Looe K… LK      200 2017-06-20 15:33:00 2017-06-20  2017 2017-06-01
 2 WS17170   Looe K… LK      200 2017-06-20 15:33:00 2017-06-20  2017 2017-06-01
 3 WS17170   Looe K… LK      200 2017-06-20 15:33:00 2017-06-20  2017 2017-06-01
 4 WS17170   Looe K… LK      200 2017-06-20 15:33:00 2017-06-20  2017 2017-06-01

# ℹ 17 more variables: time_gmt <time>, lat_in <dbl>, lon_in <dbl>,
#   maximumDepthInMeters <dbl>, volume_filt_cubic_m <dbl>, taxa <chr>,
#   scientificname <chr>, lifeStage <chr>, ind_m3 <dbl>,
#   number_ind_sample <dbl>, kingdom <chr>, phylum <chr>, class <chr>,
#   order <chr>, family <chr>, genus <chr>, taxa_level <chr>




Code
dat_lowest_200 %>%
    summarise(
    .by = c(station, site, year, date_month, taxa_level),
    # ind_m3 = mean(ind_m3)
    ind_m3 = sum(ind_m3)
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    .by     = c(site, date_month),
    total   = sum(ind_m3),
    percent = ind_m3 / total
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    taxa_level = fct_reorder(taxa_level, percent, .fun = mean),
    date_month2 = fct_reorder(format(date_month, "%b %Y"), date_month)
  ) %>%
  ggplot(
    aes(x = date_month2, y = percent, fill = taxa_level, 
        color = after_scale(prismatic::clr_darken(fill, 0.3))
        )
         ) +
  geom_col() +
  geom_point(aes(y = total / 10000), color = "red", fill = "white", shape = 21) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = -0.005, linewidth = rel(1)) +
  labs(
    y = NULL,
    x = NULL,
    fill = str_to_sentence(const$taxa_lvl),
    title = NULL
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    expand = c(0, 0),
    labels = scales::label_percent(),
     sec.axis = sec_axis(transform = ~ .x * 10000, 
                         name = expression(paste("# Individual m")^-3)
                         )
  ) +
  scale_fill_viridis_d(option = "A") +
  guides(fill = guide_legend(ncol = 1, byrow = TRUE)) +
  theme_classic() +
  facet_wrap(~station, ncol = 1, strip.position = "top") +
  theme(
    legend.box.background = element_rect(color = "grey"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.line.x.bottom = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y.right     = element_text(color = "red"),
    axis.title.y.right    = element_text(color = "red"),
    axis.ticks.y.right    = element_line(color = "red")
  )

title: MBON Zooplankton Quick Visual
text:
7.2 500 μm


Code
dat_lowest_500 <-
  dat %>%
  filter(
    !str_detect(site, "9B") # site
    # str_detect(site, const$site) # site
    & mesh == 500        # mesh size
    & is.na(lifeStage)           # assumed na is adult
  )  %>% 
  mutate(
    site = fct(site, c("MR", "LK", "WS")),
    station = fct(station, c("Molasses Reef", "Looe Key (deep)", "Western Sambo")), 
    # select the lowest taxa name available
    taxa_level = pmap_chr(
      across(c(const$taxa_lvl)),
      \(...) {
        dat <- c(...) # combine all names into a vector
        taxa_name <- NA
        # loop through all names to select the lowest name
        for (i in seq(length(dat))) {
          if (is.na(taxa_name)) {
            taxa_name <- dat[i]
          } else {
            taxa_name
          }
        }
        return(taxa_name)
      }
    ),
  ) %T>%
  print()

title: MBON Zooplankton Quick Visual
text:
7.3 Both


Code
dat_lowest_both <-
  dat %>%
  filter(
    !str_detect(site, "9B|57") # site
    & (mesh == 200 | mesh == 500)           # mesh size
    & is.na(lifeStage)      # assumed na is adult
  )  %>% 
  mutate(
    site = fct(site, c("MR", "LK", "WS")),
    station = fct(station, c("Molasses Reef", "Looe Key (deep)", "Western Sambo")), 
    # select the lowest taxa name available
    taxa_level = pmap_chr(
      across(c(const$taxa_lvl)),
      \(...) {
        dat <- c(...) # combine all names into a vector
        taxa_name <- NA
        # loop through all names to select the lowest name
        for (i in seq(length(dat))) {
          if (is.na(taxa_name)) {
            taxa_name <- dat[i]
          } else {
            taxa_name
          }
        }
        return(taxa_name)
      }
    ),
  ) %T>%
  print()

Code
dat2 <- 
  dat_lowest_both %>%
  summarise(
    .by = c(station, site, mesh, year, date_month, taxa_level),
    # ind_m3 = mean(ind_m3)
    ind_m3 = sum(ind_m3)
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    .by     = c(site, mesh, date_month),
    total   = sum(ind_m3),
    percent = ind_m3 / total
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    taxa_level = fct_reorder(taxa_level, percent, .fun = max),
    date_month2 = fct_reorder(format(date_month, "%b %Y"), date_month)
  ) 

dat2 %>%
  filter(mesh == 200) %>%
  ggplot(
    aes(x = date_month2, y = percent, fill = taxa_level, 
        color = after_scale(prismatic::clr_darken(fill, 0.3))
        )
         ) +
  geom_col() +
  geom_point(aes(y = (total / 10000)), color = "black", fill = "white", shape = 21) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = -0.005, linewidth = rel(1)) +
  labs(
    y = NULL,
    x = NULL,
    fill = str_to_sentence(const$taxa_lvl),
    title = NULL
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    expand = c(0, 0),
    labels = scales::label_percent(),
    sec.axis = sec_axis(transform = ~ .x * 10000)
  ) +
  scale_fill_viridis_d(option = "A") +
  guides(fill = guide_legend(ncol = 1, reverse = TRUE)) +
  theme_classic() +
  facet_grid(station~mesh, scales = "free") +
  theme(
    legend.box.background = element_rect(color = "grey"),
    axis.text.x           = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    panel.spacing.y       = unit(0.75, "lines"),
    panel.spacing.x       = unit(0.75, "lines"),
    axis.line.x.bottom    = element_blank(),
  )

    expand = c(0, 0),
    labels = scales::label_percent(),
    sec.axis = sec_axis(transform = ~ .x * 1000 )
  ) +
  scale_fill_viridis_d(option = "A") +
  guides(fill = guide_legend(ncol = 1, reverse = TRUE)) +
  theme_classic() +
  facet_grid(station~mesh, scales = "free") +
  theme(
    legend.box.background = element_rect(color = "grey"),
    axis.text.x           = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    panel.spacing.y       = unit(0.75, "lines"),
    panel.spacing.x       = unit(0.75, "lines"),
    axis.line.x.bottom    = element_blank(),
  )

title: CSV checker
text:
setup
library(ggplot2)
library(here)
library(skimr)




load file
data <- read.csv(here("data/zoo_data_pre_obis_merg.csv"))




Print basic diagnostic information
# print(head(data))     # View first few rows
# print(str(data))      # Structure of the data frame
# print(summary(data))  # Statistical summary of the data
#  Use skimr to provide a detailed summary
skimmed_data <- skimr::skim(data)
print(skimmed_data)

column-specific checks:


diagnose char column that should be numeric
colname <- "lat_in"
# Convert row to numeric. Non-convertible entries will become NA
numeric_colname <- paste(colname, "_as_numeric", sep = "")
data[[numeric_colname]] <- as.numeric(as.character(data[[colname]]))

# Check for NAs which indicate problematic entries
num_non_numeric <- sum(is.na(data[[numeric_colname]]))

# Print the number of non-numeric rows
print(paste("Number of non-numeric rows in 'NumericColumn':", num_non_numeric))


[1] "Number of non-numeric rows in 'NumericColumn': 0"


diagnose char column that should be numeric
# Optional: To see which rows are non-numeric
problematic_rows <- data[is.na(data[[numeric_colname]]), ]
print(problematic_rows)

<0 rows> (or 0-length row.names)


Looking at a histogram of individualCount we can see that rows generally have low numbers of individuals:


Histogram of a numeric column
hist(data$individualCount)









A scatter plot between the ind_m3 and individualCount shows that rows with more individuals also have a higher density of individuals per volume.


Scatter plot between two variables
plot(data$individualCount, data$ind_m3, main = "Scatter plot", xlab = "ind_m3", ylab = "individualCount")
